_comment: Phase B CODING tier — real merged aios PRs whose held-out test is ISOLATABLE (pure-logic pytest, no DB/network/docker). Each item: the PR/issue description = the task; the merge commit's test file = the held-out pass@1 oracle. The candidate model is shown the source file(s) at base_parent_sha and must produce the corrected source; the scorer applies it, drops in the held-out test, and runs ONLY that test with PYTHONPATH forcing the patched tree. Provenance per item: pr, issue, base_parent_sha (the patch applies onto this), merge_sha, src_files (candidate edits these), test_files (held-out oracle). headroom HIGH = subtle bug a single model may miss; LOW = mechanical. Selected + verified by a recon agent (all pass green at base+merge-source+merge-test in <3s standalone). Repo: /Users/tom/code/aios (eumemic/aios).

items.task: In the route-attenuation logic, _meet_http_server maps each route path_pattern to its first declared route via setdefault, so when a server has two routes sharing a pattern with disjoint methods, the second's verbs get silently demoted to deny-all, eroding granted authority and breaking the attenuate(x,x)==canonicalize(x) law. Fix it so a child grant is the UNION of same-pattern routes' methods (a _join dual to _meet, where None means top/all-methods and [] means bottom/none).

In the _tool_lifecycle asynccontextmanager, the tool_execute_start span append happens in __aenter__ before the try/finally; a CancelledError there escapes with no result appended and no sweep, so the call stays unresolved and the ghost sweep later emits a misleading 'may have completed'. Fix: catch CancelledError on that append, write a dedup-guarded 'cancelled' result, fire the tail sweep, and re-raise.

gh_paginated follows Link: rel="next" but on hitting max_pages with a next page still dangling, it returns the partial list as if complete (a silent list under-count, e.g. reporting 'all checks green' off page 1). Fix: raise a distinct cannot-determine error ONLY on the ceiling-with-dangling-next case, still returning cleanly on a complete walk.

At the litellm boundary, a generic 'except Exception' routes EVERY failure into the [2,8,30,120]s backoff ladder, including terminal errors (400/context-exceeded/content-policy/401/403/404/422) that retry can never fix, burning ~160s and up to 4 doomed billable calls. Add a predicate for terminal litellm classes that routes them straight to the errored-latch, fail-safe so a misclassified transient never skips retries.

The gate-reaper/telemetry dead-men call list_runs/get_run from inside a workflow run, but those are registered session-only, so they always return 'not callable from a workflow run' and both detectors are silent no-ops. Make them run-callable, account-scoped to the calling run's own account (no launching session), with errors-as-values per the run-tool contract.

_snapshot_with_bytes silently drops any walked memory file whose read_bytes() raises OSError, so an unreadable-but-present file looks absent and the diff treats it as a deletion, wiping durable version history. Fix: keep the path present in both snapshots with a stable content-independent sentinel digest so an unreadable-in-both file compares equal, handled at both the deleted and modified diff loops.

shape_call_result reads only result.content and never structuredContent; a spec-compliant MCP server (2025-06-18) returning its payload only in structuredContent yields {"content":""}, a silent empty-success the model can't retry. Fix: when content yields nothing, serialize structuredContent; also propagate outputSchema to the model.

stub_missing_reasoning_content unconditionally writes reasoning_content="" on every assistant turn, but it exists only for one provider quirk and directly contradicts _strip_to_spec earlier in the same pipeline (which strips it for non-thinking targets); the two passes fight. Fix: gate the stub on the target's supports_thinking capability.

_encode_with_transparency assumes a PNG-saveable/palette-convertible image, but a grayscale+alpha PNG decodes to Pillow mode 'LA', which convert('P', ADAPTIVE) rejects with a bare ValueError; on an oversized such image it escapes the guard and 500s the connector inbound before dedup-ack, causing an infinite poisoned retry loop. Fix: normalize non-canonical transparency modes (e.g. LA) before encode.

_validate_cron_expression rejects grammar-valid crons that have no occurrence within a 1-year horizon, but a valid leap-day cron '0 0 29 2 *' has an 8-year gap across the non-leap century skip (e.g. 2096 to 2104) and is wrongly 422-rejected. Widen the validation horizon to cover the maximum legitimate gap so valid leap-day crons are accepted.

The spend gate is reactive and keyed on a flat per-account meter, so an ancestor's limit can be blown by cumulative subtree spend while each individual row stays under. Add a PRE-FLIGHT admission gate (before the model call) keyed on the rolled-up WITH RECURSIVE subtree sum, reusing the spend_cap_exceeded latch; leave the existing post-call warning unchanged.

Persist provider error detail (exception class + HTTP status + message) on the errored-turn span when retries exhaust and child_errored latches; currently only {is_error:true} is saved. Thread a diagnostic dict through the error-span append.

is_safe_url() (the SSRF pre-flight) does a blocking socket.getaddrinfo() called un-awaited from two async handlers, stalling the event loop. Wrap both call sites in asyncio.to_thread, mirroring the existing in-repo precedent.

The read tool inlines sandbox images for vision models but its description (READ_DESCRIPTION and the path param string) only documents text-file behavior, so agents never discover image-inlining. Update the description to document image-inlining, the size cap, and the fallback behavior.
